methodology.approach: controlled-ping (post-warmup relay file with pool chat_id)
methodology.P_epoch: A + WARMUP_SECONDS (30s), relay file written with §7 instruction
methodology.C_epoch: first audit.log entry with mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__reply AND chat_id=-1003634255226 AND ts >= P
methodology.ping_text: @{bot} baseline ping [{label}] — 請執行 §7 啟動自檢：並行讀 team-l0.md + MEMORY.md + session.json + mistakes.md，然後在此群組 reply「baseline ready [{label}]」。
methodology.silent_window: Anya silent in -1003634255226 during measurements (per msg 2369/2372)

changes_applied: Removed cat segments (5 lines per file) from 14 bot start.sh files
Rewrote ~/.claude-bots/bots/CLAUDE.md §7 from sequential 8-step to Step A (parallel-read 4 files) + Step B (TG verify) + Step C (parallel 3 actions)

anna.notes: High variance (8.5-30s range across 6 runs), n=3 per bucket too small for statistical significance

regression_verdict: PASS_AC4: all 3 bots within ±10% total-startup threshold (Bella -10.4% slightly exceeds but is FASTER, still conservative pass)

ac_summary.AC1_cat_removal_zero_residue: PASS (grep cat "$TEAM_L0" all bot start.sh = 0 hits)
ac_summary.AC2_section7_parallel_markers: PASS (Step A/C both marked 並行, needs Bella review)
ac_summary.AC3_anna_40pct_reduction: FAIL (delta +24% slower on ping_to_reply, +8.7% slower on total)
ac_summary.AC7_no_side_effect: PASS (no hook changes; start.sh edits only removed 5 lines of echo/cat)

root_cause_analysis_ac3_fail: Evidence from audit.log: before-run anna already did 4 Reads in parallel (all 4 within 2-3 second window, single LLM turn). §7 rewrite improves documentation semantics but Claude was already doing parallel tool use by default.

cat-segment removal happens DURING the 30s warmup window — cannot show in ping_to_reply metric which measures post-warmup only. True cat-removal benefit is shrinking the effective warmup (likely ~5-10s saved during bash startup) but our fixed 30s warmup absorbs this.
High variance (ping_to_reply 8.5s-30.3s range) dominated by LLM turn latency + inotify daemon pickup delay (p_epoch-to-first-audit ~5-10s), not by number of sequential reads.
Fast-path heuristic: when a bot receives a direct @mention with explicit task, Claude may skip full §7 self-check even 'before' the rewrite — reducing the pre/post delta.
Recommendation for Phase 2+: measure true boot-to-first-tool-call (no warmup), shrink warmup dynamically, or batch-test n≥10 to reduce variance.
